message:
feat(server): add retention engine, health endpoints, tool metrics, and mail UI module

Major server expansion with six new modules and comprehensive test coverage:

Retention engine (retention.rs): configurable message retention policies
with age-based and count-based expiry. Runs as a background task that
periodically scans for expired messages and performs soft-deletes,
preserving thread integrity while reclaiming storage.

Ack TTL (ack_ttl.rs): acknowledgement time-to-live enforcement —
automatically marks unacknowledged messages past their TTL as expired,
sending notification messages to original senders when ack deadlines
are missed.

Cleanup (cleanup.rs): periodic housekeeping for orphaned attachments,
expired file reservations, and stale session data. Coordinates with
the retention engine to avoid conflicting cleanup operations.

Mail UI (mail_ui.rs): extracted mail dashboard route handlers into a
dedicated module for better separation of concerns — handles inbox,
thread, message detail, search, and compose views.

Static files (static_files.rs): embedded static asset serving for the
dashboard UI (CSS, JS, icons) with ETag-based caching and gzip
compression support.

Tool metrics (tool_metrics.rs): per-tool invocation metrics collection
and dashboard endpoint — tracks call counts, latency percentiles, error
rates, and payload sizes with configurable time windows.

Server lib (lib.rs): extensive routing additions integrating all new
modules, health check endpoints (/health, /ready, /metrics), structured
HTTP request logging, and graceful shutdown coordination.

Templates: fix thread view template rendering edge case.

Tests:
- health_endpoints.rs: test /health and /ready endpoint responses
- http_logging.rs: verify structured log output format and fields
- workers.rs: test background worker lifecycle and shutdown behavior
- ui_markdown_templates.rs: expand template rendering coverage

Cargo.toml: add dependencies for metrics collection and static serving.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
